sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Real Betis, currently sitting at 6th place in the league with 57 points, host bottom-placed Real Valladolid, who are struggling at 20th with only 16 points, in Round 33 of La Liga at the Benito Villamarin stadium in Seville. This matchup pits a team in solid form against one desperate for points to climb out of the relegation zone. The referee for this game, Francisco Hernandez Maeso, averages 5.0 yellow cards per match, suggesting a potentially feisty encounter.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head Encounters
sections.text: In their last 5 matchups, Real Betis have secured 2 wins, while Real Valladolid have managed 1 win, with the remaining 2 games ending in draws. Their most recent clash on January 11, 2025, saw Real Valladolid edge out a 1-0 victory at home, with Lucas Rosa earning Player of the Match honors with a Sofascore rating of 8. Historically, head-to-head games have often been low-scoring, with 5 out of their last 7 meetings producing under 2.5 goals.

sections.subtitle: Real Betis' Key Performers
sections.text: Real Betis boast some standout players, including Isco, who ranks 5th in the league for average Sofascore rating at 7.56 and has contributed 8 goals and 6 assists this season. Giovani Lo Celso is another creative force in midfield, leading the league in times fouled per game at 2.7 and overperforming his expected goals by 3.3696 with 8 goals scored. Antony, with a strong rating of 7.52, adds depth to the midfield, having netted 4 goals and provided 2 assists.

sections.subtitle: Real Betis' Team Strengths
sections.text: Real Betis rank 5th in the league for total goals scored with 52 and are particularly strong in headers, ranking 2nd with 11 headed goals. Their pressing game is impressive, ranking 3rd for possessions won in the final third per game at 4.441, while they also excel defensively, leading the league in interceptions per game at 9.47 and ranking 2nd in tackles per game at 17.08. Additionally, they average 5.2 corners per game, placing them 4th in the league.

sections.subtitle: Real Valladolid's Struggles and Key Players
sections.text: Real Valladolid are in dire form, with a streak of 6 consecutive losses and 13 games without a win, alongside failing to keep a clean sheet in their last 13 matches. Mamadou Sylla is their top scorer with 5 goals, closely followed by Raul Moro with 4 goals and 4 assists. Defensively, Adam Aznou stands out, ranking 4th in the league for aerial duels won at 77.8%, while Darwin Machis also impresses in the air, ranking 5th with 75% aerial duels won.

sections.subtitle: Historical Standout for Real Betis
sections.text: In the last 5 matches against Real Valladolid, William Carvalho has been a standout for Real Betis, boasting an average Sofascore rating of 7.44 across 3 starts. He has scored 2 goals in these encounters, proving to be a consistent threat against this opponent despite not contributing any assists in those games.

sections.subtitle: Team Streaks and Tactical Insights
sections.text: Real Betis have failed to keep a clean sheet in their last 3 games but have been the first to score in 4 of their last 5 matches, showing their ability to start strong. Real Valladolid, on the other hand, have conceded first in 9 of their last 10 games, indicating defensive vulnerabilities. Both teams have seen both teams scoring in recent matches, with Betis in 6 of their last 7 games and Valladolid in 6 of their last 8.

sections.subtitle: Real Betis' Missing Players
sections.text: Real Betis will be without several players, including defender Diego Llorente, who has scored 2 goals this season with an average rating of 7.01, and midfielder Marc Roca, who also has 2 goals and 2 assists with a rating of 7.06. Forward Chimy Avila, another missing piece, has contributed 2 goals this campaign, which could affect Betis’ depth in attack.

sections.subtitle: Scoring Patterns and Timing
sections.text: Real Betis tend to score heavily in the latter stages of matches, with 12 goals (23.8% of their total) coming between the 76th and 90th minutes. Real Valladolid also peak late, with 5 goals (27.7% of their total) in the same 76-90 minute bracket, but they struggle early, scoring just 1 goal in the first 15 minutes across the season. This suggests Betis may dominate early while both sides could push for late goals.
